rationale: The subject property's main hip roof (upper darker-shingle section within the orange dashed boundary) displays moderate shadow casting on its leeward planes relative to the building footprint, consistent with a mid-range pitch. The ridge is visibly set inward from the eave perimeter but not dramatically so, and the visible roof surface area from this near-nadir view is proportionally moderate — neither the broad flat appearance of a shallow 4:12 nor the compressed, shadow-dominated look of a steep 8–10:12. Neighboring homes in this Humble, TX suburban development show comparable dark-shingle hip roofs with similar ridge-to-eave geometry, reinforcing a 6:12 estimate. The lighter lower section of the dashed box appears to be an under-construction or low-slope accessory area and was excluded from the dominant pitch assessment. Geographic context (greater Houston area, warm climate, suburban tract construction) further supports 6:12 as the most probable pitch.
